editorial.title: 정치 중립 책임 망각한 국회의장 후보들
editorial.body: (사설) 22대 국회 하반기 국회의장 선출 과정에서 민주주의 원칙이 실종됐다는 비판이 제기됐다. 국회의장이 초당적 중립성을 지켜야 함에도 민주당 후보들은 정파적 이해와 강경한 권리당원 표심에 집중했다. |
summary: 22대 국회 의장 선출 과정에서 민주주의 원칙이 사라졌다.
민주당 후보들이 초당적 중립성을 외면했다.
정파적 이해와 강경한 표심에만 집중한 상황이다.
editorial.pullQuote: 국회의장이 초당적 중립성을 지켜야 함에도 민주당 후보들은 정파적 이해에 집중했다.
editorial.stance: 국회의장 후보들은 정치적 중립성이라는 기본 책임을 되찾아야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: Presidential candidates for National Assembly speaker forget duty of political neutrality
summaryTr: Democratic principles have disappeared in the process of electing the 22nd National Assembly speaker.
Democratic Party candidates turned their backs on bipartisan neutrality.
The situation is focused solely on partisan interests and hardline voter sentiment.
editorial.bodyTr: The selection process for the National Assembly Speaker in the second half of the 22nd National Assembly has drawn criticism that democratic principles went missing. Despite the Speaker's obligation to maintain bipartisan neutrality, Democratic Party candidates focused on factional interests and the strong sentiments of party members asserting their rights.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 민주당 후보들의 '정파적 이해'와 '강경한 표심'을 비판하며 기관의 중립성과 민주주의 원칙 준수를 강조하는 법치주의적 프레이밍으로, 집권당의 정치적 행동을 견제하는 보수적 입장

editorial.title: [사설] 기업 초과이익 환원 ‘국민배당금’ 운 띄운 청와대 정책실장
editorial.body: 입력 종합 26면 김용범 청와대 정책실장이 인공지능(AI) 시대에 기업의 초과이익을 사회에 환원하는 ‘국민배당금’이란 화두를 던졌다. 지난 11일 페이스북에 올린 글에서 “AI 시대의 메모리·인프라 수요가 장기 구조 변화라면 한국은 지속해서 초과 이윤을 생산하는 ‘기술독점 경제’에 가까운 구조로 이동할 가능성이 있다”면서다. AI 인프라 공급망에서 (한국의) 전략적 위치가 만들어낸 구조적 호황이 역대급 초과 세수로 이어진다면 그것을 ‘국민배당금’으로 활용해 양극화 문제 해결에 쓰자는 주장도 했다. 김 실장의 제안은 논쟁적인 동시에 위험하다. 지나치게 낙관적인 인식에 기반하고 있기 때문이다. AI발 반도체 호황의 지속 가능성을 장담할 수만은 없는 데다 미국과 중국 등 주요국이 맹렬하게 추격해 오고 있다. 초격차 유지를 위한 전략 없이 장밋빛 전망만 늘어놓는 것이 대통령의 최고위 경제 참모의 역할인가. 개별 기업 이익의 ‘사회 환원’이나 ‘국민 배당’이라는 용어에 담긴 반시장적 뉘앙스도 극히 우려스럽다. 그는 “AI 인프라 시대의 과실은 특정 기업만의 결과가 아니고, 반세기에 걸쳐 전 국민이 함께 쌓아 온 산업 기반 위에서 나온다”며 “과실의 일부는 전 국민에게 구조적으로 환원돼야 한다”고 했다. 그의 발언이 알려진 뒤 코스피는 장중 5% 이상 급락했다. 블룸버그는 “기업 횡재세가 아닌 초과 세수 활용이라는 당국자의 설명이 나온 뒤 낙폭을 회복했다”고 지적했다. 김 실장의 설명대로 ‘국민배당금’이 반도체 호황으로 예상되는 엄청난 초과 세수의 사용 방안을 설계하자는 것이라면 국민적 합의에 따라 나랏빚을 갚거나 지속 가능한 성장을 위한 투자와 양극화 해소를 위한 보편적 복지까지 쓰임새를 고민하면 된다. 굳이 농어촌 기본소득이나 노령연금 강화 등 사용처를 미리 정해 놓고 ‘국민배당금’이라는 이름표까지 붙일 필요는 없다. 이재명 대통령은 어제 “포퓰리즘적 긴축재정론의 함정에 빠져서는 안 된다”고 강조했다. 이런 분위기에서 나온 청와대 정책실장의 ‘국민배당금’ 제안은 기본소득 등 현금 살포 명분을 만들기란 오해를 사기 쉽다. 경제를 진짜 살리려면 객관적 현실에 기반해 시장경제 질서를 왜곡하지 않는 접근이 필요하다.
editorial.pullQuote: AI 인프라 시대의 과실은 특정 기업만의 결과가 아니고, 반세기에 걸쳐 전 국민이 함께 쌓아온 산업 기반 위에서 나온다.
summary: 청와대 정책실장이 AI 시대 기업 초과이익을 '국민배당금'으로 환원하자고 제안했다.
발언 직후 코스피가 5% 급락하며 시장이 반시장적 정책으로 해석했다.
편집진은 초과세수 활용은 필요하나 포퓰리즘식 현금 살포 명분이 되면 안 된다고 지적했다.
editorial.stance: 초과세수 활용은 논의하되, 시장경제 원리를 훼손하는 포퓰리즘식 접근은 피해야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: Presidential Chief Policy Advisor Floats 'National Dividend' Plan to Return Corporate Windfall Profits to Citizens
summaryTr: The Presidential Chief Policy Officer proposed returning corporate excess profits in the AI era as 'national dividends' to the public.
Immediately after the remarks, the Kospi plunged 5 percent as markets interpreted the proposal as anti-market policy.
The editorial board noted that while utilizing excess tax revenue is necessary, it should not become a pretext for populist cash handouts.
editorial.bodyTr: Presidential policy chief Kim Yong-beom has raised the concept of a "citizens' dividend," wherein corporations return excess profits to society in the age of artificial intelligence. In a Facebook post on the 11th, he stated: "If the demand for memory and infrastructure in the AI era represents a long-term structural shift, Korea may move toward a structure closer to a 'technology monopoly economy' that continuously generates excess profits." He argued that if structural prosperity created by Korea's strategic position in the AI infrastructure supply chain leads to record-breaking excess tax revenue, that money should be utilized as a "citizens' dividend" to address wealth inequality. Chief Kim's proposal is both contentious and problematic. It rests on an excessively optimistic outlook. The sustainability of semiconductor booms driven by AI cannot be guaranteed, particularly as major nations like the United States and China are aggressively catching up. Is it the role of the president's top economic advisor to offer only rosy projections without a strategy to maintain technological superiority? The anti-market undertones embedded in the terminology of individual companies "returning profits to society" or "citizens' dividends" are deeply concerning. He stated: "The fruits of the AI infrastructure era are not the result of any single company alone, but emerge from the industrial foundation built collectively by our entire nation over half a century. Part of these fruits must be structurally returned to all citizens." After his remarks became public, the KOSPI plummeted more than five percent intraday. Bloomberg noted that the decline recovered after government officials explained the concept as utilizing excess tax revenue rather than imposing a windfall tax on corporations. If Chief Kim's proposal, as he explained, means designing how to use enormous excess tax revenue anticipated from the semiconductor boom, then pursuing national consensus on paying down national debt, investing in sustainable growth, and providing universal welfare to address inequality would suffice. There is no need to predetermine specific uses such as basic income for rural areas or enhanced pensions, nor to attach the label "citizens' dividend" to the plan. President Lee Jae-myung yesterday stressed: "We must not fall into the trap of populist fiscal austerity." The presidential office policy chief's "citizens' dividend" proposal in this climate is easily misconstrued as justification for distributing cash handouts like basic income. To truly revitalize the economy, we need an approach grounded in objective reality that does not distort the market economy order.

leanScore.rationale: 기업 초과이익 환원을 '반시장적'으로 비판하고 '시장경제 질서 왜곡' 우려를 강조하며 현금 살포를 포퓰리즘으로 경고하는 시장주의적 입장

editorial.title: Fired upon in Hormuz
editorial.body: Seoul must identify attacker, respond firmly, seek guarantees for safe passage The South Korean government said Sunday that the explosion and fire aboard the Namu, a cargo ship operated by South Korea’s HMM, in the Strait of Hormuz on May 4 were “caused by an external strike.” A Foreign Ministry spokesperson said, “The investigation confirmed that two unidentified airborne objects struck the stern of the HMM vessel at intervals of about one minute.” The two consecutive strikes strongly suggest that the attack was deliberate. Had the attack struck slightly lower, the vessel might have sunk. This is a shocking incident comparable to the 2010 sinking of the Cheonan by North Korea. The ministry said it remains unclear whether Iran was behind the attack, adding that investigators would conduct further analysis of debris recovered from the scene. The ministry summoned the Iranian ambassador to South Korea to its headquarters and briefed him on the investigation findings. Although the ministry stopped short of officially identifying the attacker, that the incident occurred in the inner Strait of Hormuz, an area within Iran’s sphere of influence, and that it summoned the Iranian ambassador after the investigation, strongly suggest Iran as being behind the attack. Speculation emerged early on that Iran was behind the attack, as it has previously carried out strikes in the region not only against US naval vessels, but also against ships from other countries. Shortly after the Namu incident, US President Donald Trump said that the incident was caused by an Iranian attack. However, a senior presidential official said, “The cause of the explosion is still unknown. It is not yet clear whether the vessel was struck.” The official added, “The matter first needs to be investigated, and that will take quite some time.” The South Korean government avoided the term "attack." We cannot but ask why it had to. It is questionable if intelligence was not shared. Iran’s state-run Press TV reported on Wednesday that a South Korean ship became a target after violating its newly established maritime regulations. The following day, however, the Iranian Embassy in Seoul dismissed the report, saying the article did not represent the official position of the Iranian government. In the end, the South Korean government’s investigation concluded that the incident was in fact caused by an attack. It also raises the possibility that Iran’s hard-line military, which reportedly operates on a different line from the Iranian government, may have carried out the attack upon its own initiative. Foreign Minister Cho Hyun said Monday that a closer examination is needed regarding the strike. While conducting a thorough investigation is the right approach in principle, the government should not drag the process out unnecessarily. Not only the South Korean public, but also the rest of the world is watching how the South Korean government responds. Now that it has been confirmed that the vessel was attacked, it is essential to determine whether the airborne objects were drones or missiles and, above all, who was responsible for the attack. The attack on the Namu is a grave incident that threatened the safety of the South Korean people. The government must respond firmly. An issue involving a threat to public safety must not be brushed aside without a clear resolution. If further investigation confirms that Iran was behind the launch of the projectiles, the South Korean government should lodge a strong protest with Tehran and demand measures to prevent a recurrence. Given that South Korean vessels are still effectively trapped in the Persian Gulf, Seoul should obtain assurances from Iran that they will be able to pass through the strait safely. It should also consider participating actively in international coalition initiatives for Hormuz. editorial.pullQuote: The attack on the Namu is a grave incident that threatened the safety of the South Korean people.
summary: South Korea confirms external strike hit cargo ship HMM Namu in Strait of Hormuz on May 4.
Two consecutive airborne strikes suggest deliberate attack; Iran suspected but not officially named.
Government must identify attacker, respond firmly, and secure safe passage guarantees for Korean vessels.
editorial.stance: South Korea must decisively investigate the attack, demand accountability from Iran, and protect its vessels in contested waters.
summaryTr: 한국이 5월 4일 호르무즈 해협에서 화물선 HMM 나무호가 외부 공격을 받았음을 확인했다.
연속된 공중 공격은 의도적 공격을 시사하며, 이란이 용의선상에 있으나 공식 지명되지 않았다.
정부는 공격자를 규명하고 단호히 대응하며 한국 선박의 안전한 통항을 보장받아야 한다.
editorial.bodyTr:
서울은 공격자를 특정하고 단호히 대응하며 안전한 통행 보장을 확보해야 한다

남한 정부는 지난 5월 4일 호르무즈 해협에서 한국 HMM이 운영하는 화물선 나무호에서 발생한 폭발과 화재가 "외부 공격으로 인한 것"이라고 일요일 발표했다. 외교부 대변인은 "조사 결과 정체불명의 비행 물체 두 개가 약 1분 간격으로 HMM 선박의 선미에 충돌한 것으로 확인됐다"고 말했다. 이처럼 연이은 두 번의 공격은 이것이 의도적인 공격임을 강하게 시사한다. 만약 공격이 조금 더 낮은 위치에서 발생했다면 선박이 침몰했을 수도 있었다. 이는 2010년 북한의 천안함 침몰 사건에 비견할 만한 충격적인 사건이다. 외교부는 이란이 공격의 배후인지 여부는 여전히 불명확하다고 말했으며, 현장에서 수거한 파편에 대한 추가 분석을 실시할 것이라고 덧붙였다. 외교부는 이란 대사를 본부로 소환해 조사 결과를 설명했다. 외교부가 공격자를 공식적으로 특정하지는 않았지만, 사건이 이란의 영향력 범위 내인 호르무즈 해협 내부에서 발생했으며 조사 후 이란 대사를 소환한 사실은 이란을 강하게 시사한다. 이란은 과거 미국 해군 함정뿐만 아니라 다른 나라의 선박도 공격해온 바 있어, 초기부터 이란의 배후 가능성에 대한 추측이 제기됐다. 나무호 사건 직후 도널드 트럼프 미국 대통령은 이 사건이 이란의 공격으로 인한 것이라고 언급했다. 그러나 대통령실의 고위 관계자는 "폭발의 원인은 여전히 미상이며, 선박이 공격받았는지 여부도 아직 명확하지 않다"고 말했다. 그 관계자는 "먼저 조사가 이루어져야 하며, 그에는 상당한 시간이 걸릴 것"이라고 덧붙였다. 남한 정부는 "공격"이라는 용어를 피했다. 우리는 그 이유가 무엇인지 묻지 않을 수 없다. 정보 공유가 이루어지지 않았던 건 아닐까 하는 의문이 든다. 이란 국영 프레스TV는 수요일 남한 선박이 새로 수립한 해상 규정을 위반해 목표가 됐다고 보도했다. 그러나 다음날 서울 이란 대사관은 해당 기사가 이란 정부의 공식 입장을 대변하지 않는다며 이를 부인했다. 결국 남한 정부의 조사는 사건이 실제로 공격으로 인한 것이라고 결론내렸다. 또한 이란의 강경파 군부가 이란 정부와 다른 입장에서 독자적으로 공격을 감행했을 가능성도 제기된다. 조 현 외교부 장관은 월요일 공격에 대한 더 자세한 검토가 필요하다고 말했다. 철저한 조사를 진행하는 것이 원칙적으로 올바른 접근이지만, 정부는 불필요하게 과정을 끌어서는 안 된다. 남한 국민뿐만 아니라 전 세계가 남한 정부의 대응을 지켜보고 있다. 선박이 공격받았다는 것이 확인된 만큼, 비행 물체가 드론인지 미사일인지, 그리고 무엇보다 공격의 책임자가 누구인지 규명하는 것이 필수적이다. 나무호에 대한 공격은 남한 국민의 안전을 위협한 심각한 사건이다. 정부는 단호하게 대응해야 한다. 국민 안전 위협과 관련된 사안은 명확한 해결 없이 넘어갈 수 없다. 추가 조사를 통해 이란이 발사체 발사의 배후임이 확인된다면, 남한 정부는 테헤란에 강력한 항의를 제기하고 재발 방지 조치를 요구해야 한다. 남한 선박들이 여전히 사실상 페르시아만에 갇혀 있다는 점을 감안할 때, 서울은 해협의 안전한 통행을 보장할 것을 이란으로부터 확보해야 한다. 또한 호르무즈 해협에 대한 국제 연합 이니셔티브에 적극적으로 참여하는 것도 검토해야 한다.

editorials.title: Fired upon in Hormuz
editorials.body: Seoul must identify attacker, respond firmly, seek guarantees for safe passage The South Korean government said Sunday that the explosion and fire aboard the Namu, a cargo ship operated by South Korea’s HMM, in the Strait of Hormuz on May 4 were “caused by an external strike.” A Foreign Ministry spokesperson said, “The investigation confirmed that two unidentified airborne objects struck the stern of the HMM vessel at intervals of about one minute.” The two consecutive strikes strongly suggest that the attack was deliberate. Had the attack struck slightly lower, the vessel might have sunk. This is a shocking incident comparable to the 2010 sinking of the Cheonan by North Korea. The ministry said it remains unclear whether Iran was behind the attack, adding that investigators would conduct further analysis of debris recovered from the scene. The ministry summoned the Iranian ambassador to South Korea to its headquarters and briefed him on the investigation findings. Although the ministry stopped short of officially identifying the attacker, that the incident occurred in the inner Strait of Hormuz, an area within Iran’s sphere of influence, and that it summoned the Iranian ambassador after the investigation, strongly suggest Iran as being behind the attack. Speculation emerged early on that Iran was behind the attack, as it has previously carried out strikes in the region not only against US naval vessels, but also against ships from other countries. Shortly after the Namu incident, US President Donald Trump said that the incident was caused by an Iranian attack. However, a senior presidential official said, “The cause of the explosion is still unknown. It is not yet clear whether the vessel was struck.” The official added, “The matter first needs to be investigated, and that will take quite some time.” The South Korean government avoided the term "attack." We cannot but ask why it had to. It is questionable if intelligence was not shared. Iran’s state-run Press TV reported on Wednesday that a South Korean ship became a target after violating its newly established maritime regulations. The following day, however, the Iranian Embassy in Seoul dismissed the report, saying the article did not represent the official position of the Iranian government. In the end, the South Korean government’s investigation concluded that the incident was in fact caused by an attack. It also raises the possibility that Iran’s hard-line military, which reportedly operates on a different line from the Iranian government, may have carried out the attack upon its own initiative. Foreign Minister Cho Hyun said Monday that a closer examination is needed regarding the strike. While conducting a thorough investigation is the right approach in principle, the government should not drag the process out unnecessarily. Not only the South Korean public, but also the rest of the world is watching how the South Korean government responds. Now that it has been confirmed that the vessel was attacked, it is essential to determine whether the airborne objects were drones or missiles and, above all, who was responsible for the attack. The attack on the Namu is a grave incident that threatened the safety of the South Korean people. The government must respond firmly. An issue involving a threat to public safety must not be brushed aside without a clear resolution. If further investigation confirms that Iran was behind the launch of the projectiles, the South Korean government should lodge a strong protest with Tehran and demand measures to prevent a recurrence. Given that South Korean vessels are still effectively trapped in the Persian Gulf, Seoul should obtain assurances from Iran that they will be able to pass through the strait safely. It should also consider participating actively in international coalition initiatives for Hormuz. summaryTr: 한국이 5월 4일 호르무즈 해협에서 화물선 HMM 나무호가 외부 공격을 받았음을 확인했다.
연속된 공중 공격은 의도적 공격을 시사하며, 이란이 용의선상에 있으나 공식 지명되지 않았다.
정부는 공격자를 규명하고 단호히 대응하며 한국 선박의 안전한 통항을 보장받아야 한다.

editorial.titleTr: Semiconductor boom tax windfall to be returned to people... dark shadow of populism looms
summaryTr: The presidential office has proposed distributing excess semiconductor tax revenues to citizens as dividend payments.
The aim is to share the fruits of growth in the AI era, but concerns about populism loom large.
The sudden push via social media amid controversy over semiconductor performance bonuses is problematic.
editorial.bodyTr:
Kim Yong-beom, head of policy at the presidential office, has proposed a "universal dividend" scheme to redistribute part of the windfall tax revenue from the semiconductor boom to all citizens. The idea is premised on the notion that all people should share in the fruits of growth in the AI infrastructure era. This can be understood as raising a social issue amid ongoing controversy over semiconductor performance bonuses. However, there is a whiff of populism in the argument, and it is problematic that a high-ranking presidential official suddenly floated such a radical idea through social media without sufficient prior discussion or deliberation.

The semiconductor industry is indeed enjoying unprecedented prosperity. Last year, semiconductor exports exceeded 100 billion dollars, and tax revenues have surged accordingly. It is natural that society engages in serious debate about how to use these fruits wisely for future growth and national welfare. The government's interest in this matter is understandable.

Yet the universal dividend proposal raises several concerns. First, there is no guarantee that the current semiconductor boom will continue indefinitely. The industry is vulnerable to global economic fluctuations and technological shifts. If windfall revenues are permanently allocated to dividends, it could create fiscal difficulties down the road. Second, one-time cash handouts to citizens do little to address structural economic challenges. Investments in education, infrastructure, and research and development would be far more effective in building sustainable growth foundations.

Most importantly, major policy decisions should emerge through democratic deliberation, not through sudden announcements via social media by government officials. The presidential office should present a comprehensive plan, solicit expert opinions, and engage in thorough parliamentary discussion before proceeding. Only then can policies command public trust and achieve their intended purpose.

summaryTr: The site selection process for 27 nationwide transmission line projects will be suspended for one month.

Amid an AI and semiconductor hegemony war, expanding power infrastructure has become a national mission where every second counts.
editorials.bodyTr: The site selection process for 27 nationwide transmission line projects will be halted for a month. This comes after the government accepted demands from civic groups and residents opposed to transmission tower construction to pursue "procedural improvements." In the midst of AI and semiconductor hegemony wars, expanding power infrastructure is a national mission where every second counts. Yet the reality that our economy's vital "energy highway" is once again floundering in a quagmire of civil complaints and conflict is frustrating.
